business_interpretation: Customer retention is a meaningful business risk.
recommended_action: Monitor churn rate monthly and segment churn by customer group.

key_finding: DevTools has the highest churn rate at 30.97%.
business_interpretation: DevTools customers may require stronger feature fit and technical product depth.
recommended_action: Review DevTools feature usage and collect segment-specific feedback.

key_finding: Event-acquired customers have the highest churn rate at 30.21%.
business_interpretation: Event-acquired customers may need better qualification and onboarding.

key_finding: Active and churned subscriptions show very similar product usage levels.
business_interpretation: Churn is not explained by low usage alone; feature gaps, pricing and support context may matter more.
recommended_action: Combine usage analysis with churn reasons and customer feedback.

key_finding: Support experience is broadly similar, but churned accounts show slightly higher escalations.
business_interpretation: Complex or escalated support issues may matter more than total ticket volume.
recommended_action: Create an escalation-risk flag for customer success teams.

key_finding: Churned subscriptions represent $14.15M ARR at risk.
business_interpretation: Enterprise and high-value accounts should be prioritised for retention.
recommended_action: Build early-warning tracking for high-MRR and Enterprise customers.
